article_title: NVIDIA Hand-Delivers First Vera CPUs to Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceXAI as Agentic AI Drives a New CPU Moment

content_preview.title: NVIDIA Hand-Delivers First Vera CPUs to Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceXAI as Agentic AI Drives a New CPU Moment
content_preview.summary: NVIDIA VP Ian Buck personally delivered the first production Vera CPU systems to Anthropic, OpenAI, SpaceXAI, and Oracle in California, marking the commercial arrival of NVIDIA's first purpose-built agentic AI processor.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

NVIDIA Vice President of Hyperscale and High-Performance Computing Ian Buck made personal deliveries of the first Vera CPU systems to Anthropic, OpenAI, SpaceXAI, and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure across four California locations on May 17 and 20, 2026, according to the [NVIDIA Blog](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/vera-cpu-delivery/). The hand-deliveries mark the commercial arrival of Vera — NVIDIA's first custom CPU built specifically for agentic AI workloads and, as [previously repor...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean, well-sourced News article covering the first production Vera CPU deliveries. Good use of three complementary sources: NVIDIA Blog for the delivery narrative, NVIDIA Newsroom for technical specs, Tom's Hardware for the single-SKU and financial framing. Appropriate 'What We Don't Know' section acknowledging the limits of NVIDIA's own benchmark data.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources verified from snapshots. source-0 (NVIDIA Blog): confirmed Ian Buck deliveries to Anthropic San Francisco, OpenAI Mission Bay, SpaceXAI Palo Alto (May 17) and Oracle Santa Clara (May 20), Bradbury 'Scaling compute is an important accelerant' quote, Batta 'hundreds of thousands' quote, Buck 'Agentic AI is creating a new CPU moment' quote. source-1 (NVIDIA Newsroom): confirmed Jensen Huang 'Vera is arriving at a turning point' quote verbatim, 1.2 TB/s memory bandwidth, 1.8 TB/s NVLink-C2C (7x PCIe Gen 6), 22,500 concurrent environments per 256-unit rack, Spatial Multithreading, Gallego 5.5x latency quote, Cazes TACC quote, Truell Cursor quote. source-2 (Tom's Hardware): confirmed 88 active cores, 91 total with 3 for redundancy, Ian Buck 'We only are going to build one Vera CPU [SKU]' quote, Jensen Huang 'multi-billion dollar business' quote. Note: Tom's Hardware source contains a likely typo referencing 'Intel' instead of 'NVIDIA' for the redundancy logic — article correctly uses NVIDIA.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Article correctly reports 91 total cores / 88 active (3 for redundancy) from Tom's Hardware. Jensen Huang quote about 'multi-billion dollar business' is accurate (source says 'multi-billion dollar business for us'). All performance specs (1.2 TB/s memory, 1.8 TB/s NVLink, PCIe 7x comparison) confirmed by NVIDIA Newsroom.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Performance claims are attributed to NVIDIA and noted as company statements; external user quotes provide third-party perspective. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately cautions that no independent benchmarks exist yet.
editor_notes.originality: Not a duplicate. The February article covered the original NVIDIA Rubin/Vera announcement; this covers the actual first-delivery event in May 2026. Article appropriately cross-references the prior coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE: Well-sourced news article with all 3 sources verified and all key claims confirmed. Clean submission with no integrity issues.
